title: South African police chief suspended over $20m health contract

title: Negotiations that enable Israel’s land-grabs

originalTitle: Iranian FM Araghchi to visit Pakistan as talks with US set to resume
title: Big Important Grown-ups Meet Far Away!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi Super Kids! It’s your friendly news reporter, here to tell you about exciting things happening all around our big, beautiful world. Today, we are going to learn about some very important grown-ups who are meeting far, far away from us. It’s a bit like when your head teacher meets with the head teachers from other schools to talk about how to make school even better for you!

Big News from Far Away Lands!

Imagine a country called Pakistan. It’s a lovely country, just like ours, with many people and amazing places. Right now, Pakistan is doing something very special. They are hosting a big meeting! This meeting has very important people from two other countries. One country is called Iran, and the other is called the United States of America, or USA for short. They are coming together to talk. **Did you know?** Pakistan is a country in Asia! It has very tall mountains and deserts.

Who is Visiting Pakistan?

A very important person from Iran is visiting Pakistan. His name is Mr. Araghchi. He is like a super-duper important helper for the President of Iran. Grown-ups call him a "Foreign Minister." That just means he helps his country talk to other countries. He is coming to Pakistan to meet with important people from the USA. It’s like when your parents visit their friends – they go to their friend’s house to talk and spend time together!

Why Do Important People Meet?

When grown-ups from different countries meet, it’s often to talk about important things. They talk about how to be friends, how to help each other, and how to solve problems without fighting. These meetings are called "talks." Imagine if you and your friend had a little disagreement. You would sit down and talk about it, right? That’s what these important grown-ups do, but on a much bigger scale for entire countries! They want to make sure everyone can live safely and happily. **Did you know?** When leaders talk, it helps countries be friends and work together!

What Happens When VIPs Visit?

When very important people, sometimes called VIPs, visit a city, special things happen. The city where these talks are happening in Pakistan is a big one. It's like two cities joined together, called a "twin-city." To make sure everyone is safe, especially the important visitors, there is extra security. This means there might be more police or special guards around. They want to make sure nothing bad happens during these important meetings.

Roads Closed and Courts Quiet

Because the important people are visiting and having their talks, some roads in the twin-city have to be closed for a little while. This is like when your street might be closed for a big parade or a special event. It helps keep everyone safe and makes sure the visitors can travel easily. Also, a place called the "court" might be quiet. Courts are where grown-ups go to talk about rules and solve big problems. But during these important visits, some courts might close for a day or two. This is so everyone can focus on the big talks and keep the city safe. **Did you know?** Closing roads for special guests is a way to keep them very safe!

What About Shops and Work?

When roads are closed and things are very busy with important visitors, it can sometimes make things a bit tricky for the people who live in the city. Imagine if you couldn't go to your favourite shop because the road was closed, or if your parents found it harder to go to work. This is what we call "economic strain" for grown-ups. It means it might be a little harder for people to buy and sell things, or for grown-ups to do their jobs easily. But it’s usually just for a short time, while the big meetings are happening. People understand that these talks are very important for peace.

Why is This Important for Everyone?

These talks are super important! When countries talk instead of disagreeing, it helps everyone. It’s like when you and your friends work out a problem; everyone feels better afterward. These important people are trying to find ways for their countries to get along better. If countries are friendly and work together, it means less worry for everyone and more chances for people everywhere to live in peace. Imagine a world where all countries are friends – how wonderful that would be!

Being Friends is Good!

So, even though these talks are happening far away, they remind us of a very important lesson: talking and being kind to each other is always the best way. Whether it’s you and your friends in the playground, or big leaders from different countries, talking helps us understand each other and build a better, happier world. Let’s all remember to talk things out and be friendly! That's all for today's news, super kids! See you next time!
summary: Important leaders from Iran and the USA are meeting in Pakistan to talk about being friends and solving problems, which means some roads are closed and things might be a little tricky for people living there, but it's all for peace.
whyMatters: These important meetings help countries be friends and work together, so everyone can live in peace and be happy, just like when you talk to your friends to solve a problem.

originalTitle: What’s been happening during the Lebanon ceasefire?
title: What’s been happening during the Lebanon ceasefire?
content: Trump has extended the Lebanon ceasefire by 3 weeks. But Israel has been accused of using the truce to further its war.

title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
content: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

title: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'
content: An internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a change in position in retaliation for the UK not joining the Iran war.

originalTitle: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested

originalTitle: Prestianni gets six-match ban for homophobic conduct
title: Prestianni gets six-match ban for homophobic conduct
content: Gianluca Prestianni is given a six-game ban by Uefa for homophobic conduct during Benfica's Champions League knockout play-off matched with Real Madrid in February.

title: Who will win the title? Reporters, fans and pundits make their predictions
content: Will it be Manchester City or Arsenal for the Premier League title? BBC Sport gets pundits, reporters and fans to predict each of their remaining fixtures - and who will win the league.

originalTitle: Why Wembley is special, for FA Cup semi-finals too
title: Why Wembley is special, for FA Cup semi-finals too
content: Tony Pulis explains why getting to Wembley is not just special for any manager, it is still hugely important for players, fans and all the staff at any club.
